title: Maxillaria lutea
taxonomy: Maxillaria lutea is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. This classification places it within the angiosperm division of the plant kingdom, which includes flowering plants. The Orchidaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Maxillaria lutea belonging to the subfamily Spiranthoideae. Within the Spiranthoideae subfamily, Maxillaria lutea is classified as a member of the tribe Maxillarieae. The specific classification of Maxillaria lutea is thus: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Angiospermophyta, Class: Monocotyledonae, Order: Asparagales, Family: Orchidaceae, Subfamily: Spiranthoideae, Tribe: Maxillarieae.

intro: Maxillaria lutea, also known as the yellow Maxillaria orchid, is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, where it is used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Maxillaria lutea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and bronchitis.
